title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Go Semantics Case: Http2 Config Capacity
summary: A public server uses package defaults, has zero timeouts, trusts unlimited headers, enables cleartext HTTP/2 accidentally, serves weak TLS, and terminates active requests on deploy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo http2 config capacity increment in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Http2 Config Capacity · Theory Http2 Config Capacity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9871 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Http2 Config Capacity contract for the supplied data.
